title: Sweet Irish Kidney StirFry
description: A comforting, lowcarb Irish stirfry featuring lamb kidneys, sweet bell pepper, and a splash of Irish whiskey.
instructions: Trim and pat the lamb kidneys dry, then slice them thinly into bite‑sized pieces.
Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat.
Add the sliced onions and sauté for 2–3 minutes until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the sliced bell pepper and mushrooms; stir‑fry for 4–5 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the kidney slices to the skillet, stirring gently to avoid breaking them up.
Season with chopped thyme, smoked paprika, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Pour in 1 tbsp of low‑sodium soy sauce and 1 tbsp Irish whiskey, stirring to coat all the ingredients.
Cook for an additional 3–4 minutes, ensuring the kidneys are browned on the edges but still tender and pink inside.
Squeeze 2 tbsp of fresh lemon juice over the mixture and stir to brighten the flavors.
Remove from heat, sprinkle chopped parsley, and serve immediately, optionally with a side of steamed greens or a low‑carb cauliflower mash.
